title: Capture a consistent logical backup
description: Use a single-transaction mysqldump or mydumper so an InnoDB dump is point-in-time consistent without locking writes.
body: Use when taking a logical backup of an InnoDB database that stays online. Actions: run mysqldump with --single-transaction --source-data=2 so the dump runs inside one consistent snapshot and records the binlog position, or use mydumper for parallel chunks on large datasets; never add --lock-all-tables on an InnoDB-only schema because it needlessly blocks writes. Verify the dump restores into a scratch instance and the recorded binlog coordinates let you replay forward. Validation signals include the dump header carrying a CHANGE MASTER position and a test restore completing without errors. Risk boundary: --single-transaction does not protect against concurrent DDL, so freeze schema changes during the dump and confirm no ALTER ran mid-backup.
